GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Estee Lauder's cult serum just got even better", "Who knew you could improve on perfection? Estee Lauder did, and the result is the new and improved Advanced Night Repair serum.", "Australia's best-selling anti-ageing facial serum – Estee Lauder Advanced Night Repair – is a beloved formula worldwide. Famed for its age-fighting, skin-repairing, glow-making prowess, it's been almost 40 years, since the cult little brown bottle first arrived (and stayed) in beauty cabinets everywhere. Ever a pioneer, the serum (first called Night Repair when it launched in 1982) was the first ever skincare product to include the super hydrator, hyaluronic acid. Tweaks to the iconic formula have only been done four times in its history. The latest advancements have been seven years in research and development, and also include a move from plastic to chic, amber, recyclable glass. To celebrate the most recent breakthrough in the always innovative formulation which provides accelerated skin repair and collagen production, as well as more hydration and more protection (from modern ails like pollution and blue light), Vogue gathered a roster of ANR's most ardent fans. Style makers like Deborah Symond O'Neil, Gab Waller and Jess Pecoraro have been ANR enthusiasts for years. "I saw a transformation. It reduced redness, improved the texture and the clarity of my skin, and evened the tone. Everything was just hydrated and glowing," says Symond O'Neil. Skin therapist Diandra Politano says she discovered ANR courtesy of particularly radiant clients. "The latest formulation has increased antioxidants which protect skin from environmental stressors like pollution and blue light. And powerful new ingredients which work to boost collagen production, making skin look firm and plump," she says. Oil-free, the super serum has become an icon for good reason. It's a democratic beauty buy – one that suits all skin types. "It's potent, but it's so well tolerated by everyone," adds Politano. Food stylist and author Donna Hay and Fluidform Pilates founder Kirsten King, both use ANR in ways other than its name would suggest. Over winter, Hay slathers it on her skin before a cold morning run. King blends hers with Double Wear Foundation for sheer coverage that lasts all day. "It stays dewy all day – it's magic," says King."
